# MDE v1.6.5

## Features

- Added Automation Center management entry points for workspace and global automation flows, including enable, disable, rename, delete, and executor editing flows.
- Reworked the Automation Center workspace filter into global automation flows, automation-flow enabled workspaces, and automation-flow not enabled workspaces, with workspace groups collapsed by default.
- Replaced automation-flow checkboxes with pressed and unpressed menu-item states so selected flows are controlled consistently from the menu UI.
- Added Automation Center run history below the workspace filter panel, with enough room to inspect previous runs and open a parse/process details popup.
- Added Explorer integration for automation-flow scope management so the workspace Explorer window is opened or focused and its automation-flow section is expanded.
- Added startup linking for the bundled `automation-flow-helper` skill into supported AI tool skill directories.

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed automation-flow management gaps where valid flows could not be enabled, disabled, renamed, deleted, or edited from the Automation Center.
- Fixed workspace grouping so workspaces that contain automation-flow files appear under the enabled section, while other workspaces remain available but unselected.
- Fixed root `.mde` visibility so a workspace-level `.mde` folder is visible in Explorer and editor link directory pickers while nested hidden `.mde` folders remain hidden.
- Improved automation-flow diagnostics so settings that are not in the task queue can point users toward concrete repair actions instead of only showing an abstract count.
- Removed the Automation Center agent-chat entry and legacy automation-flow context-menu button surface that no longer matched the management workflow.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Maintenance

- Updated the workspace release metadata to `1.6.5` for the root workspace, desktop app, and changed private `@mde/agent-chat` and `@mde/automation-flow` packages.
- Added executor parsing, ownership, global-flow application, run-history, skill-catalog, skill-linking, workspace visibility, integration, and E2E coverage for the Automation Center release.
- Stabilized the Automation Center lifecycle E2E by waiting for the selected task Flowline before starting the next run.
- Added repo-local Codex skill links for `automation-flow-helper` and `execute-picked-task`.

## Documentation/User Manual

- Updated the Chinese user manual for Automation Center management, workspace behavior, and component naming changes.
- `pnpm run docs:build` is required for this release because `user-manual/` changed.

## Verification

- Release verification for `v1.6.5`:
  - `pnpm run lint`
  - `pnpm run typecheck`
  - `pnpm run build`
  - `pnpm run test:unit` (90 files / 593 tests)
  - `pnpm run test:integration` (71 files / 230 tests)
  - `pnpm exec playwright test --config apps/desktop/playwright.config.ts apps/desktop/tests/e2e/automation-center.e2e.test.ts -g "covers a valid automation-flow across lifecycle"` (1 test)
  - `pnpm run test:e2e` (69 Playwright tests passed / 1 skipped, plus editor performance smoke)
  - `pnpm --filter @mde/agent-chat test` (12 files / 59 tests)
  - `pnpm --filter @mde/automation-flow test` (11 files / 71 tests)
  - `pnpm run docs:build`
  - `git diff --check`

## Artifacts

- GitHub Actions Release workflow publishes macOS DMG/ZIP artifacts and Windows NSIS installer artifacts for `v1.6.5`.
- macOS signing and notarization remain optional for the no-paid-account release path; unsigned macOS artifacts are expected when signing secrets are not configured.
